ogTitle: North Kununoppin vs North Lake: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Kununoppin and North Lake.
heading: North Kununoppin vs North Lake — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Kununoppin, Western Australia and North Lake, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Lake has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to North Kununoppin (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Kununoppin or North Lake safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Kununoppin has a flood risk score of 25/100, while North Lake scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Kununoppin scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. North Lake scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Kununoppin or North Lake?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Lake has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
